title: 10 best herbal capsules for pimples

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion capsules helps with pimples because they contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce redness and swelling. The herb has been traditionally used to treat skin conditions, including acne and other inflammatory skin issues. The capsules also help to detoxify the body by stimulating liver function, which can help to remove toxins that contribute to acne. Additionally, dandelion may help to balance hormones, which is often linked to hormonal breakouts. By taking herbal dandelion capsules, individuals may experience fewer and less severe pimples.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory effects: Dandelion's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as sesquiterpenes and flavonoids, help reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with pimples.

Antibacterial properties: The herb contains antimicrobial compounds like taraxasterol and taraxeryl acetate that can combat acne-causing bacteria, reducing the growth of Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes) and other bacteria that contribute to pimple formation.

Antioxidant activity: Dandelion's antioxidants, including vitamins A, C, and E, help neutralize free radicals that can damage skin cells and contribute to acne, promoting overall skin health and reducing the appearance of pimples.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in Dandelion, such as quercetin, have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce redness and inflammation associated with pimples.
Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids like caffeic acid and chlorogenic acid in Dandelion may exhibit antimicrobial properties, helping to prevent bacterial infections that can cause pimples.

Taraxasterol: Taraxasterol, a triterpene found in Dandelion, has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help soothe and calm the skin, reducing the appearance of pimples.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are rich in inulin, a prebiotic fiber that can aid digestion and have anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: They contain taraxasterol, a compound with potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help soothe skin issues.
Seeds: They are a good source of taraxasterol and other compounds with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help protect the skin from damage.
remedy_recipe: Collect fresh taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers in sufficient quantity for the intended dosage.
Dry the collected taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers using a low-temperature oven for 2 hours at 150 degrees fahrenheit.
Grind the dried taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 2 minutes.
Mix the powder with a binding agent such as gelatin in a ratio of 1:1 and fill into empty gelatin capsules.
Store the herbal taraxacum officinale capsules in a cool dark place for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe capsules helps with pimples because they contain a potent blend of anti-inflammatory and antibacterial compounds that work synergistically to combat acne-causing bacteria. The aloe gel extract in these capsules soothes and calms the skin, reducing redness and swelling associated with pimples. Additionally, the antioxidants present in aloe help to neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate acne, promoting healthier skin and reducing the appearance of pores. As a result, herbal aloe capsules provide a natural and effective way to combat pimples and achieve clearer, more radiant skin.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Herbal aloe capsules contain anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with acne, making them an effective treatment for inflammatory pimples.

Antibacterial: The antibacterial properties in herbal aloe capsules help kill bacteria like Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes) that can cause acne, reducing the risk of infection and promoting healing.

Wound-healing: Herbal aloe capsules contain compounds that accelerate wound healing by stimulating collagen synthesis, improving tissue elasticity, and enhancing skin hydration, which helps to reduce the appearance of pimple scars and dark spots.
remedy_constituents: Aloe-emodin: Aloe-emodin is an anthraquinone with anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which helps to reduce the redness and swelling associated with pimples.

Chrysophanic acid: Chrysophanic acid is a phenolic compound that has been shown to exhibit anti-acne properties, inhibiting the growth of Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes) bacteria, a common cause of acne.

Fucosylated xylooligosaccharides: Fucosylated xylooligosaccharides are complex carbohydrates that have been found to have anti-inflammatory and wound-healing properties, which can help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing the formation of pimples.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The gel extracted from the leaves is commonly used due to its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce pimple inflammation and promote healing.
Gel from Leaves (also known as Sap or Juice): This is the most used part of Aloe vera as it is rich in vitamins, minerals, and antioxidants that help reduce pimple redness and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Crush 5-7 grams of dried aloe vera powder using a mortar and pestle for about 5 minutes.
Add 10-15 grams of vegetable cellulose powder to the crushed aloe vera powder and mix thoroughly for 3 minutes.
Gradually add 20-30 ml of purified water to the mixture and stir until it forms a thick paste in 2 minutes.
Fill a capsule machine with the prepared paste and fill the capsules to the recommended dosage in 5 minutes.
Seal the filled capsules using a capsule sealing machine and store them in an airtight container immediately.

remedy_properties: Inflammation reduction: Kansas coneflower's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as cichoric acid and chicoric acid, help reduce inflammation in the skin, which can alleviate symptoms of acne like redness and swelling.

Antimicrobial activity: The plant's bioactive compounds, including echinacoside and polysaccharides, exhibit antimicrobial properties that can help combat Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes), a bacteria that contributes to the development of pimples.

Immune system modulation: Kansas coneflower extracts have been shown to modulate the immune response by increasing the production of cytokines and activating immune cells, which can help reduce inflammation and prevent bacterial infections that can lead to acne.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower capsules helps with pimples because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The herb, also known as Echinacea, contains compounds that help reduce redness and swelling associated with acne-prone skin. Its antimicrobial properties also combat bacteria that can cause breakouts, while its antioxidants neutralize free radicals that contribute to inflammation. By addressing these underlying causes, Kansas coneflower capsules can help regulate skin health and reduce the appearance of pimples, leaving skin looking clearer and more balanced.
remedy_constituents: Alkaloids: Alkaloids present in Echinacea angustifolia, such as echinatin and echinacoside, have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can help reduce inflammation and combat bacterial infections that contribute to the development of pimples.

Iridoid glycosides: Iridoid glycosides, including echinacoside and cichoric acid, found in Echinacea angustifolia, have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that can help soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and swelling associated with pimples.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in Echinacea angustifolia, such as kaempferol and quercetin, have potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help protect the skin from oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to the formation of pimples.
remedy_parts: Roots: They contain a high concentration of compounds with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help soothe and reduce acne.
Leaves: They are rich in antioxidants and other bioactive compounds that can help reduce inflammation and prevent further breakouts.
Barks: They have been traditionally used to treat skin conditions, including acne, due to their astringent and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of echinacea angustifolia roots with a digging tool and store them in a cool dry place.
Dry the harvested roots in a food dehydrator at 40°c for 48 hours to preserve their medicinal properties.
Grind 50 grams of the dried root into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix 250 mg of the powdered echinacea angustifolia with 500 mg of a natural filler like cornstarch in a glass bowl.
Fill 50 empty gelatin capsules with the echinacea angustifolia mixture and seal them using a capsule sealer.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Germander capsules helps with pimples because of its natural antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The herb, also known as Teucrium chamaedrys, contains compounds that effectively combat Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes), the bacteria responsible for causing acne. Additionally, germander's anti-inflammatory effects help reduce redness and swelling associated with pimples, allowing the skin to heal faster and promoting a clearer complexion. By addressing both the underlying causes of acne, germander capsules can provide a natural solution for reducing pimple breakouts.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Germander capsules contain compounds like geraniol, linalool, and limonene that exhibit antimicrobial activity against bacteria such as Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes), which is a primary cause of acne, helping to reduce the severity of pimples.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Germander capsules contain flavonoids like kaempferol and quercetin, which have anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce redness, swelling, and inflammation associated with pimples, promoting healing and reducing scarring.

Antioxidant properties: Germander capsules are rich in antioxidants like vitamin C, beta-carotene, and lycopene, which can help neutralize free radicals that damage skin cells, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation that contributes to pimple formation.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic diterpene has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce redness and inflammation associated with pimples.
Ursolic acid: As a triterpenoid, ursolic acid has potent anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which can help prevent the growth of bacteria that can cause pimples and reduce inflammation.

Carvacrol: This phenolic terpene has antimicrobial properties, which can help inhibit the growth of bacteria that can cause pimples, and its anti-inflammatory properties can also help reduce redness and swelling.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Known for their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce redness and inflammation associated with pimples.
Flowers: Used for their astringent and antibacterial properties, which help to dry out and prevent the growth of bacteria that cause pimples.
Roots: Utilized for their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce inflammation and prevent the spread of bacteria that cause pimples.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the dried leaves of teucrium chamaedrys at a dosage of 250 grams.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a mill.
Mix the powdered leaves with a binding agent such as gelatin at a dosage of 5 grams.
Fill a capsule with the mixture using a manual capsule filler.
Dry the capsules for 24 hours at room temperature to prevent moisture accumulation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort capsules helps with pimples because they contain hyperforin, a bioactive compound that has potent anti-inflammatory properties. This natural ingredient reduces redness and swelling associated with acne, allowing the skin to heal faster and more effectively. Additionally, St John's Wort capsules may also help to reduce stress and anxiety, common triggers of hormonal imbalances that can contribute to acne breakouts. By addressing these underlying issues, herbal supplements can provide a holistic approach to managing pimples and promoting healthier, clearer skin.
remedy_properties: Hypolipidemic effect: St John's Wort has been shown to lower triglycerides and LDL cholesterol, which can help reduce inflammation and prevent clogged pores that lead to acne.
Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants in St John's Wort help neutralize free radicals that can damage skin cells and contribute to oxidative stress, a known contributor to pimples and acne.

Anti-inflammatory properties: St John's Wort's anti-inflammatory compounds have been shown to reduce inflammation in the skin, which can help alleviate redness, swelling, and pain associated with pimples.
remedy_constituents: Hyperforin: This phenolic compound has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce redness and inflammation associated with pimples.
Naphthodianthrones (e.g., Hypericin and Pseudohypericin): These compounds have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help prevent bacterial overgrowth and reduce acne symptoms.

Flavonoids (e.g., Quercetin and Kaempferol): These polyphenolic compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help protect the skin from oxidative stress and reduce inflammation that can lead to pimples.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used to make capsules for pimples due to their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties.
Leaves: They are used to make capsules for pimples due to their ability to reduce redness and inflammation in the skin.
Seeds: They are used to make capsules for pimples due to their astringent and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-60 grams of dried hypericum perforatum flowers and leaves from a trusted source.
Grind the dried hypericum perforatum into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 1 minute.
Mix 3-5 grams of the powdered hypericum perforatum with 5 grams of gelatin in a bowl.
Fill a capsule machine with the gelatin mixture and press the capsules according to manufacturer's instructions.
Store the herbal hypericum perforatum capsules in an airtight container in a cool dry place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage capsules helps with pimples because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The antioxidants present in sage work to reduce inflammation in the skin, which is often a primary cause of acne. Additionally, sage has antibacterial qualities that help combat Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes), a bacteria that contributes to the development of pimples. By reducing inflammation and killing bacteria, sage capsules can help clear up blemishes and reduce their occurrence, promoting healthier and clearer skin.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Herbal sage capsules contain antioxidants that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the skin, which can contribute to acne and pimple formation.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Sage has anti-inflammatory compounds like rosmarinic acid, which can reduce redness and swelling associated with pimples, making them less noticeable and painful.

Antibacterial properties: Herbal sage capsules contain antibacterial agents that can help combat Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes), a type of bacteria that can contribute to the formation of acne and pimples.
remedy_constituents: Trigonelline: Trigonelline has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce redness and swelling associated with pimples.
Rosmarinic acid: Rosmarinic acid has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce bacterial growth and inflammation that contribute to pimples.
Carvacrol: Carvacrol has antibacterial properties, which can help control the growth of bacteria such as Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes), a common cause of pimples.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used for their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties to help reduce acne and pimple inflammation.
Flowers: Used for their antiseptic and antifungal properties to help prevent infections and promote healing of acne and pimples.
Seeds: Used for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties to help reduce inflammation and promote skin health, reducing the appearance of pimples.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500 grams of dried salvia officinalis leaves and flowers from a clean environment.
Dry the harvested plant material for 24 hours in a temperature-controlled room at 40 degrees celsius.
Grind the dried plant material into a fine powder using a high-speed blender for 10 minutes.
Mix 100 grams of the powdered plant material with 500 grams of vegetable cellulose powder in a large bowl.
Fill 1000 empty gelatin capsules with the mixture and seal them using a manual capsule filler.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle capsules helps with pimples because they contain anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce redness and swelling associated with acne. The capsules also help to regulate hormonal imbalances, which can contribute to acne breakouts. Additionally, the antioxidants present in stinging nettle may help to combat free radicals that can damage skin cells and cause inflammation, leading to clearer and healthier-looking skin. By taking herbal supplements regularly, individuals can promote a balanced complexion and reduce the appearance of pimples.
remedy_properties: Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Stinging nettle capsules contain compounds like sesquiterpenes and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory effects that can reduce redness, swelling, and inflammation associated with acne and pimples.

Antioxidant Activity: Nettle capsules are rich in antioxidants like vitamin C, beta-carotene, and lutein, which help neutralize free radicals that can damage skin cells, leading to the formation of acne lesions.

Astringent and Toner-like Effects: The astringent properties of nettle extracts can help tighten pores, reduce oil production, and balance the skin's pH, making it less prone to clogged pores and pimples.
remedy_constituents: Alkaloids: Alkaloids, such as 5-hydroxytryptamine and stinging nettle lectin, found in Urtica dioica may help reduce inflammation and prevent bacterial growth that can contribute to pimple formation.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids, including quercetin and kaempferol, present in Urtica dioica have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and inflammation associated with pimples.

Saponins: Saponins, such as urticoside, found in Urtica dioica may help to reduce the production of sebum, an oily substance that can clog pores and lead to pimple formation, while also exhibiting antimicrobial properties to prevent infection.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part due to their high content of salicylic acid and other compounds that help to reduce inflammation and acne.
Roots: The roots of Urtica dioica are used to create capsules that help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and inflammation associated with pimples.
Stems: The stems of Urtica dioica are also used to make capsules that contain anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce the severity of pimples and prevent scarring.
remedy_recipe: Harvest a sufficient amount of fresh urtica dioica leaves and stems from a clean area.
Dry the harvested urtica dioica in a low-temperature oven at 40 degrees celsius for 24 hours.
Crush the dried urtica dioica into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix the urtica dioica powder with a binding agent like gelatin in a ratio of 1:1 and fill empty capsules.
Store the urtica dioica capsules in an airtight container in a cool dry place for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile capsules helps with pimples because they contain apigenin, an antioxidant that soothes inflammation and reduces redness. Additionally, chamomile's anti-bacterial properties help to eliminate acne-causing bacteria, such as Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes), from the skin. By reducing inflammation and killing bacteria, chamomile capsules can help to clear up pimples and prevent future breakouts. Regular use may also improve skin clarity and reduce the appearance of scars and hyperpigmentation left by acne.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Chamomile contains anti-inflammatory compounds like apigenin and luteolin, which can help reduce redness and swelling associated with pimples.

Antioxidants: Chamomile is rich in antioxidants that can neutralize free radicals and protect the skin from damage caused by environmental stressors, pollution, and UV radiation, which can contribute to pimple formation.
Antibacterial properties: Chamomile has antibacterial properties that can help combat Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes) bacteria, a common cause of acne and pimples.
remedy_constituents: Apigenin: This flavonoid has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce redness and swelling associated with pimples.
Chamazulene: This sesquiterpene lactone has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help prevent the growth of bacteria that cause acne and reduce inflammation in the skin.

α-Bisabolol: This sesquiterpene alcohol has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help soothe and calm the skin, reducing the formation of new pimples and promoting the healing of existing ones.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers are used for their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which can help to reduce redness and calm acne.
Leaves: The leaves are used for their antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and protect the skin from damage caused by pimples.
Seeds: The seeds are used for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to reduce the severity of pimples and prevent infection.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers at peak potency and store them in an airtight container.
Grind the dried flowers into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 2 minutes.
Mix 1 part of the powder with 2 parts of a binding agent like gelatin or cellulose.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the powder mixture using a capsule filler for 15 minutes.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemongrass capsules helps with pimples because of its natural antibacterial properties, which effectively combat acne-causing bacteria on the skin. The capsule's citral content reduces inflammation and redness associated with breakouts, while its antifungal properties prevent fungal infections that can lead to clogged pores. Additionally, lemongrass' antioxidant properties help soothe and calm the skin, reducing post-inflammatory hyperpigmentation and promoting a clearer complexion.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial property: Lemongrass capsules contain compounds like citral and geraniol, which have antibacterial effects, helping to reduce the growth of Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes) bacteria that can contribute to acne.
Anti-inflammatory property: Lemongrass contains flavonoids and terpenes that have anti-inflammatory properties, reducing redness, swelling, and inflammation associated with pimples and acne.
Antioxidant property: The antioxidants present in lemongrass capsules help protect the skin from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to the formation of pimples.
remedy_constituents: Citral: This terpene compound has antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce the bacterial load and inflammation associated with pimples.
Geraniol: As a phenolic compound, geraniol exhibits antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help soothe and calm the skin, reducing the appearance of pimples.
Limonene: This terpene has antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help control the growth of bacteria that cause pimples and reduce inflammation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are commonly used due to their high content of citral and geraniol, which have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties that help in treating pimples.
Stems: The stems are used to extract essential oils, particularly citral, which has been shown to reduce inflammation and combat bacteria that cause pimples.
remedy_recipe: Dry 500 grams of cymbopogon citratus leaves in a dehydrator at 40c for 6 hours.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 15 minutes.
Mix 100 grams of the powdered leaves with 50 grams of gelatin in a bowl.
Fill 1000 vegetable capsules with the gelatin mixture using a capsule filler machine.
Label and store the herbal capsules in an airtight container at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Neem capsules helps with pimples because of its natural antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The capsules contain a concentrated extract of neem oil, which penetrates deep into the skin to combat acne-causing bacteria and reduce inflammation. This helps to unclog pores, preventing future breakouts and reducing the appearance of existing pimples. Additionally, neem's antioxidant properties help to soothe and calm the skin, promoting healing and reducing scarring. As a result, herbal neem capsules can provide effective relief from acne and pimples without harsh chemicals or artificial ingredients.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial: Neem's antibacterial properties help to reduce the growth of bacteria such as Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes) that can contribute to acne and pimple formation, thereby preventing infection and inflammation.

Anti-inflammatory: Neem's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with pimples by reducing the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals in the body, allowing the skin to heal faster.

Antioxidant: Neem's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals that can damage skin cells and contribute to oxidative stress, which can lead to pimple formation; antioxidants also promote collagen production, improving skin elasticity and reducing the appearance of fine lines and wrinkles.
remedy_constituents: Limonoids: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which help to reduce inflammation and prevent bacterial infections that can cause pimples.
Sesquiterpenes: These compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation that can lead to pimple formation and acne.

Azadirachtin: This compound has anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which help to reduce inflammation and prevent bacterial infections that can cause pimples, while also promoting wound healing and skin regeneration.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Used due to their high concentration of nimbin and nimondial, which have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that help in reducing acne and pimples.

Leaves: Used because they contain azadirone and other compounds that have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help in controlling the growth of acne-causing bacteria and reducing inflammation.

Buds: Used due to their high concentration of salannin, which has anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that help in reducing acne and pimples by controlling bacterial growth and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 kilogram of azadirachta indica leaves and flowers, and dry them in the sun for 2 days.
Grind the dried azadirachta indica into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 30 minutes.
Mix 250 grams of the powder with 250 grams of gelatin and 100 grams of water to create a paste.
Fill gelatin capsules with the paste and seal them using a capsule filler machine.
Label and store the herbal azadirachta indica capsules in an airtight container in a cool dry place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eyebright capsules helps with pimples because they contain the potent herb Euphrasia officinalis, which has natural anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. These properties help to reduce redness and swelling associated with acne, while also combating bacterial infections that can cause breakouts. Additionally, eyebright's astringent properties help to dry out excess oil and tighten pores, reducing the appearance of pimples and preventing future occurrences. By targeting the underlying causes of pimples, herbal eyebright capsules provide a natural solution for clear and healthy-looking skin.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Eyebright's antioxidant compounds help neutralize free radicals that can damage skin cells and lead to inflammation, reducing the severity of pimple breakouts.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Eyebright's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and terpenes, reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with pimples by inhibiting prostaglandin production and suppressing inflammation.

Astringent and antibacterial properties: The astringent and antibacterial compounds in Eyebright help dry up excess oil and reduce the growth of bacteria that can cause pimples, promoting healthy skin and reducing acne severity.
remedy_parts: Roots: Eyebright roots are known for their anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce redness and swelling associated with acne.
Seeds: Eyebright seeds contain flavonoids that have antibacterial properties, effectively fighting off bacteria that cause pimples.
remedy_recipe: Harvest euphrasia officinalis leaves and flowers in the morning after dew has dried.
Dry the harvested material using shade drying or air circulation for 7-10 days.
Grind the dried material into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 250mg of euphrasia officinalis powder with 500mg of gelatin capsules and 50mg of magnesium stearate for filling.
Fill clean gelatin capsules with the powder mixture and seal immediately to maintain potency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold capsules helps with pimples because they contain calendula, a natural anti-inflammatory and antibacterial agent that soothes and calms the skin. By reducing redness and swelling, it helps to unclog pores and reduce acne breakouts. The antioxidants in pot marigold also help to neutralize free radicals that can cause damage to the skin, promoting healthy skin cell growth and collagen production. As a result, pot marigold capsules can help to reduce the appearance of pimples, leaving the skin looking clearer and more radiant.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial property: Calendula's antibacterial compounds, such as triterpenoids and flavonoids, help to reduce the growth of bacteria on the skin, which can contribute to acne and pimple formation.

Anti-inflammatory property: Pot marigold capsules contain anti-inflammatory agents that can help to soothe and reduce redness and swelling associated with pimples, making them less noticeable and more manageable.

Wound-healing property: Calendula's ability to promote wound healing by stimulating collagen synthesis and improving blood flow can also aid in the treatment of acne scars and post-inflammatory hyperpigmentation (PIH) caused by severe pimples.
remedy_parts: Roots: Potent anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce redness and swelling associated with pimples.
Seeds: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which can help soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh calendula flowers and dry them using shade drying or air circulation methods for 7-10 days.
Grind the dried calendula flowers into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 250mg of the powdered calendula with 500mg of rice flour in a small bowl to create a blend.
Fill #0 capsules with 1 gram of the blend using a capsule filler machine and store them in an airtight container.
Take one or two capsules orally twice daily for 6-8 weeks as needed to help alleviate pimple symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pineapple weed capsules helps with pimples because they contain salicylic acid, a natural beta hydroxy acid that effectively exfoliates the skin, unclogs pores, and reduces inflammation. The capsules also possess antibacterial properties, which help to combat P. acnes bacteria that cause acne. Additionally, pineapple weed has anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and swelling associated with pimples. By regulating sebum production and promoting healthy skin cell turnover, these capsules can lead to a clearer and more radiant complexion.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as apigenin and luteolin, can help reduce redness and swelling associated with acne.

Antibacterial properties: The flavonoids present in chamomile, including apigenin and quercetin, have been shown to exhibit antibacterial activity against certain bacteria that can contribute to acne development.

Soilant and soothing properties: Chamomile's anti-irritant and moisturizing properties can help calm and soothe the skin, reducing inflammation and promoting a healthy barrier function that may help prevent clogged pores and reduce acne severity.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, the roots help reduce redness and inflammation associated with acne.
Seeds: Containing omega-6 fatty acids, the seeds promote healthy skin and reduce sebum production, leading to clearer complexions.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh matricaria discoidea flowers and dry them thoroughly in shade for 2-3 weeks.
Grind dried matricaria discoidea flowers into fine powder using mortar and pestle for about an hour.
Mix ground matricaria discoidea powder with 200mg of gelatin and 100ml of distilled water to form a paste.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the paste mixture, making sure to leave no air pockets or excess space.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months or freeze them for longer storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Angelica capsules helps with pimples because they contain the herb angelica sinensis, which has been traditionally used to treat skin issues such as acne and rosacea. The capsules work by reducing inflammation and combating bacterial infections that can cause breakouts. Additionally, angelica's antibacterial properties help to prevent pores from becoming clogged with dead skin cells and excess oil, allowing for a clearer complexion.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Angelica's antibacterial compounds, such as volatile oils and terpenes, help combat acne-causing bacteria like Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes), reducing inflammation and preventing further breakouts.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The bioactive compounds in angelica capsules, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, have anti-inflammatory effects that can reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with pimples, promoting faster healing and resolution of acne lesions.

Antioxidant properties: Angelica's antioxidant properties help neutralize free radicals that contribute to oxidative stress, inflammation, and cell damage, which can exacerbate acne and lead to scarring, while also protecting the skin from environmental stressors.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of angelica contain a compound called bergapten, which has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, making it effective in treating pimples.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of angelica contain a high concentration of volatile oils that help to reduce inflammation and promote healing, making them ideal for treating acne-prone skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of dried angelica archangelica roots and stems from a trusted supplier.
Grind the 50g of dried angelica archangelica into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 2 minutes.
Mix 10g of the powdered angelica archangelica with 20g of gelatin in a small bowl until well combined.
Add 100ml of distilled water to the mixture and stir gently for 10 minutes to create a uniform paste.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with approximately 0.5g of the paste and seal immediately for later use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice capsules helps with pimples because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm red, swollen skin. The glycyrrhizin present in licorice root has been shown to reduce inflammation and inhibit the growth of bacteria that can cause acne. Additionally, licorice capsules have antioxidant properties that help to neutralize free radicals, which can damage skin cells and contribute to the formation of pimples. By reducing inflammation and combating oxidative stress, licorice capsules provide a natural and effective solution for clearing up pimples and promoting healthy, clear skin.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce redness and swelling associated with acne and pimples.

Flavonoids: Licorice root contains various flavonoids, such as isoliquiritigenin and licoricidin, that have been shown to exhibit antibacterial and antifungal properties, which can help combat bacterial infections that contribute to pimple formation.

Triterpenoid saponins: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help soothe and protect the skin from further damage caused by acne-causing bacteria and environmental stressors.
remedy_parts: Roots: Contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing acne inflammation and redness.
Barks: Rich in flavonoids and saponins, which have antibacterial and antifungal properties, helping to combat the underlying causes of pimples and reduce their severity.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 250 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra root powder.
Mix 125 milliliters of distilled water with 25 grams of honey in a saucepan.
Heat the mixture over medium heat for 15 minutes until it reaches 80 degrees celsius.
Steep 50 grams of loose-leaf green tea in boiling water for five minutes and then cool.
Fill empty capsules with a combination of glycyrrhiza glabra root powder and green tea extract equal to 250 milligrams.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Asiatic pennywort capsules helps with pimples because they contain a rich concentration of antioxidants, anti-inflammatory compounds, and beta-carotene that work synergistically to combat acne. The capsules' extract from the leaves of the Asian pennywort plant has been shown to reduce inflammation in the skin, unclog pores, and regulate sebum production, thereby preventing clogged pores and reducing the formation of pimples. Additionally, Asiatic pennywort's antioxidant properties help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, promoting overall skin health and clarity.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Asian Pennywort's antioxidant compounds help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to pimple formation.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The herb's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as madecassic acid, help reduce redness and swelling associated with pimples, allowing the skin to heal more quickly.
Antibacterial properties: Asian Pennywort's antibacterial compounds inhibit the growth of bacteria that can clog pores and cause acne, reducing the severity and frequency of pimples.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids that help reduce inflammation and prevent oxidative stress, making them effective in treating acne-causing bacteria.
Rhyzomes: Contain anti-inflammatory compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which soothe redness and swelling associated with pimples.
Leaves: Packed with vitamins A and C, as well as flavonoids, that help reduce inflammation, prevent scarring, and promote skin regeneration.
remedy_recipe: Dry fresh centella asiatica leaves at room temperature for 24 hours.
Grind dried leaves into fine powder using mortar and pestle.
Mix powdered centella asiatica with 200mg of gelatin in a bowl.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the herbal mixture and seal immediately.
Store the capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Skullcap capsules helps with pimples because they contain a powerful herb that has natural anti-inflammatory properties. This herb, known as Scutellaria laterifolia, is able to reduce inflammation in the skin, which is often a key factor in the development of acne and other types of pimples. By reducing inflammation, skullcap capsules can help to unclog pores and prevent future breakouts, leading to clearer, healthier-looking skin.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Skullcap's antioxidant compounds help neutralize free radicals that can damage skin cells, reducing inflammation and oxidative stress that can contribute to pimple formation.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Skullcap's flavonoids and phenolic acids have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce redness, swelling, and irritation associated with pimples.

Antibacterial properties: Skullcap's compounds, such as baicalin and wogonin, exhibit antibacterial activity against certain bacteria that can cause acne, including Pseudomonas aeruginosa and Staphylococcus aureus.
remedy_parts: Roots: Containing high amounts of bioactive compounds, Skullcap roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness and swelling associated with pimples.
Rhyzomes: The underground stems of the Skullcap plant, rhyzomes possess antibacterial properties that target acne-causing bacteria, reducing the occurrence of breakouts.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality scutellaria lateriflora leaves and flowers from a trusted source ensuring proper identification.
Dry the harvested plant material by spreading it thinly on paper bags or trays in a warm ventilated area for 1-2 weeks.
Grind dried scutellaria lateriflora into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or coffee grinder to ensure optimal potency.
Mix 250 milligrams of the ground herbal powder with 500 milliliters of distilled water in a clean glass container for 30 minutes.
Fill empty capsules with the prepared liquid extract allowing it to dry completely before storing them in an airtight container for up to one year.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Comfrey capsules helps with pimples because of its exceptional anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce redness and swelling associated with acne. The herb's allantoin content promotes tissue repair, accelerating the healing process of damaged skin. Additionally, comfrey's antimicrobial properties combat bacteria that contribute to pimple formation. By addressing both inflammation and infection, comfrey capsules provide a natural, effective solution for clearing up pimples and promoting healthy, radiant skin.
remedy_properties: Allantoin: Comfrey contains allantoin, a potent anti-inflammatory agent that can help reduce redness and swelling associated with pimples.
Ferulic acid: Ferulic acid in comfrey has antioxidant and antibacterial properties, which may help combat acne-causing bacteria and prevent damage caused by free radicals.
Rosmarinic acid: Rosmarinic acid, a polyphenolic compound found in comfrey, exhibits anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities that can help soothe and heal pimples while preventing infection.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in allantoin, a potent anti-inflammatory agent that soothes and calms acne-prone skin.
Buds: High in saponins, which have antibacterial properties that help combat pimple-causing bacteria.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of fresh symphytum officinale leaves and stems, wash them thoroughly with distilled water.
Dry the plant material in a single layer at room temperature for 7-10 days, stirring occasionally, until crumbly.
Grind 20 grams of dried symphytum officinale into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered herb with 80 grams of capsule filler material and 10 grams of silicon dioxide in a large bowl, blending well for 5 minutes.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the herbal mixture, dropping approximately 500 milligrams per capsule, storing them in an airtight container for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm capsules helps with pimples because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce redness and swelling associated with acne. The capsules also contain antioxidants that help to combat free radicals, which can contribute to skin damage and breakouts. Additionally, lemon balm's antimicrobial properties help to control bacteria growth on the skin, preventing clogged pores and reducing the occurrence of pimples. Overall, taking herbal lemon balm capsules regularly can lead to a clearer, healthier-looking complexion.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties: Lemon balm's antibacterial properties help to reduce the growth of bacteria that cause acne, while its anti-inflammatory properties reduce redness and swelling associated with pimples.
Antioxidant properties: Lemon balm's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals that can damage skin cells and contribute to the formation of pimples.

Anti-stress and mood-regulating properties: Stress is a common trigger for acne, and lemon balm's anti-anxiety and mood-regulating properties may help to reduce stress levels, which in turn can help to alleviate pimple-prone skin.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing acne inflammation and promoting healthy skin.
Leaves: Contain rosmarinic acid, which has antibacterial properties that help combat Propionibacterium acnes bacteria responsible for causing pimples.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melissa officinalis leaves by cutting them off from a healthy plant.
Dry the harvested leaves in a warm dark place for 1-2 weeks or until crispy.
Grind dried melissa officinalis leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 250mg of ground melissa officinalis with 500mg of vitamin e oil in a small bowl.
Fill empty capsules with the prepared mixture and seal them immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Goldthread capsules helps with pimples because it contains a powerful herb, Coptis chinensis, that possesses antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The capsules work to reduce the severity of acne by killing bacteria that cause breakouts, while also soothing and calming the skin. Additionally, goldthread has been shown to regulate hormone imbalances, which can contribute to acne development. By taking these capsules regularly, individuals may experience a significant reduction in the frequency and appearance of pimples.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial property: Goldthread capsules contain berberine, a natural antibacterial compound that helps kill Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes), the bacteria that causes acne.
Anti-inflammatory property: Goldthread has anti-inflammatory compounds like berberine and coptisine, which can reduce redness, swelling, and irritation associated with pimples.

Antioxidant property: Goldthread capsules contain antioxidants like quercetin and kaempferol, which help protect the skin from free radical damage caused by pollution, stress, and other environmental factors that contribute to acne.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in berberine, a compound that has antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in reducing acne and pimple inflammation.
Rhyzomes: Containing berberine and other active compounds, they help to reduce sebum production, unclog pores, and combat bacterial overgrowth that can cause pimples.
remedy_recipe: Gather ingredients including coptis chinensis rhizome 2g, honey 10%, and gelatin capsule shells.
Dry the coptis chinensis rhizome in a low-temperature oven at 40°c for 24 hours.
Grind the dried coptis chinensis rhizome into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered coptis chinensis with honey 10% and fill gelatin capsule shells to a total weight of 500mg.
Seal the filled capsules in an airtight container and store them in a cool dark place.

intro_desc: Herbal capsules for pimples are a natural supplement made from various herbs, botanicals, and plant extracts that help to reduce acne and inflammation caused by pimples. These capsules contain ingredients such as tea tree oil, turmeric, and neem, which have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that soothe the skin and prevent further breakouts. Examples of herbal capsules for pimples include products like Clear Skin Capsules and Acne Treatment Plus. By using these supplements, individuals can reduce acne scars, prevent future breakouts, and regain confidence in their appearance, ultimately improving their overall quality of life.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to pimples that are treated with herbal capsules are acne, blackheads, and whiteheads. These herbal supplements aim to reduce inflammation, prevent bacterial growth, and regulate hormonal imbalances. Capsules containing herbs like Neem, Turmeric, Ginger, and Aloe Vera have been known to effectively alleviate symptoms of these skin issues, promoting healthy skin and reducing the appearance of imperfections.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal capsules that help with pimples is Neem and Turmeric. Neem capsules have antibacterial properties that reduce inflammation and prevent bacterial growth, while Turmeric capsules contain Curcumin, which has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to reduce redness and promote healing. Additionally, taking a combination of Tea Tree Oil and Aloe Vera capsules can also be beneficial, as Tea Tree Oil has antibacterial properties and Aloe Vera soothes and calms the skin, reducing acne and promoting smooth skin.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Evidence-based complementary and alternative medicine : eCAM, herbal capsules can be beneficial for pimples due to their ability to significantly reduce inflammatory and noninflammatory acne lesions. The study, which analyzed 34 clinical trials involving 1753 participants, found that most trials showed a relevant effect of herbal medicine on acne severity, suggesting that herbal capsules can help alleviate the symptoms of pimples. Additionally, the study revealed that some medicinal plants demonstrated equal or higher efficacy compared to standard treatments, further supporting the potential benefits of herbal capsules for pimple treatment.
